---
title: "Architecture, Abstraction, and Design"
---

# Architecture, Abstraction, and Design

Range: `0001-0112`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 112   |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                | Completion | Semantic        | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ----- | ----------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0001  | [abstraction](#rune-0001)           | authored   | reviewed        | A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism. Shadow: hiding the only failure that matters.                                                                      |
| 0002  | [adapter](#rune-0002)               | authored   | reviewed        | A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another. Shadow: adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it.                                       |
| 0003  | [agent {Arch}](#rune-0003)          | authored   | generated_draft | `agent` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the agent obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0004  | [aggregate {Arch}](#rune-0004)      | authored   | generated_draft | `aggregate` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the aggregate obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 0005  | [anti-corruption layer](#rune-0005) | authored   | reviewed        | A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.                                                                                                                                                                |
| 0006  | [API gateway](#rune-0006)           | authored   | reviewed        | A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world. Shadow: a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.                                                                     |
| 0007  | [architecture](#rune-0007)          | authored   | generated_draft | `architecture` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the architecture obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 0008  | [artifact boundary](#rune-0008)     | authored   | generated_draft | `artifact boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the artifact boundary obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 0009  | [assembler](#rune-0009)             | authored   | generated_draft | `assembler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the assembler obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0010  | [backbone](#rune-0010)              | authored   | generated_draft | `backbone` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the backbone obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0011  | [backend](#rune-0011)               | authored   | generated_draft | `backend` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the backend obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0012  | [boundary](#rune-0012)              | authored   | reviewed        | The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins. Shadow: porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.                                                                                        |
| 0013  | [bounded context](#rune-0013)       | authored   | reviewed        | A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem. Shadow: false unification through shared names.                                                                            |
| 0014  | [bridge](#rune-0014)                | authored   | generated_draft | `bridge` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the bridge obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |

Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.                                                                                   |

| 0034  | [dependency inversion](#rune-0034)  | authored   | reviewed        | Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail. Shadow: concrete dependencies hard-wired into places that should remain portable.                                                                                      |

## Entries

### [0001] abstraction {#rune-0001}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism.

**Shadow:** hiding the only failure that matters.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `abstraction` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism.
- Ask the assistant to state the `abstraction` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `abstraction` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about cleanly.

### [0002] adapter {#rune-0002}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another.

**Shadow:** adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `adapter` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another.
- Ask the assistant to state the `adapter` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `adapter` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** A translator between unlike shapes that lets systems meet without infecting one another.

### [0003] agent {Arch} {#rune-0003}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `agent` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the agent obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `agent` at rune 0003 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `agent` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `agent` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the agent obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `agent` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `agent` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [0004] aggregate {Arch} {#rune-0004}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `aggregate` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the aggregate obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `aggregate` at rune 0004 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `aggregate` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `aggregate` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the aggregate obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `aggregate` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `aggregate` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [0005] anti-corruption layer {#rune-0005}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.

**Shadow:** misusing `anti-corruption layer` at rune 0005 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `anti-corruption layer` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.
- Ask the assistant to state the `anti-corruption layer` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `anti-corruption layer` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.

### [0006] API gateway {#rune-0006}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world.

**Shadow:** a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `API gateway` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world.
- Ask the assistant to state the `API gateway` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `API gateway` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** An edge threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing.

### [0012] boundary {#rune-0012}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins.

**Shadow:** porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `boundary` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins.
- Ask the assistant to state the `boundary` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `boundary` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** The line where one responsibility ends and another begins.

### [0013] bounded context {#rune-0013}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem.

**Shadow:** false unification through shared names.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `bounded context` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem.
- Ask the assistant to state the `bounded context` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `bounded context` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** A semantic fence: the same term is not forced to mean the same thing everywhere.

### [0030] coupling {#rune-0030}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.

**Shadow:** when a change in one component quietly forces coordinated edits across unrelated surfaces.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `coupling` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.
- Ask the assistant to state the `coupling` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `coupling` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere.

### [0055] interface {Arch} {#rune-0055}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.

- Name `interface` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.
- Ask the assistant to state the `interface` shadow before proposing changes.

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `wrapper` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the wrapper obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `wrapper` at rune 0112 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `wrapper` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `wrapper` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the wrapper obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `wrapper` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `wrapper` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.
